message:
security: Gemini lane 3 — wire concrete revocation lookup into enforcement pipeline (CL-L3-01)

Closes the critical security gap documented in GEMINI_LANE3_REVOCATION.md:
the canonical 11-stage enforcement pipeline's "Revocation freshness"
stage previously validated ONLY the age of the revocation list, not
whether the presented token, issuer key, node attestation, or binary
artifact had actually been revoked. Standalone hosts using the
canonical pipeline would therefore accept fully-revoked tokens as long
as their local revocation list was within the freshness window.

Changes:

- crates/fcp-host/src/enforcement.rs
  - EnforcementContext / EnforcementContextBuilder expanded with
    token_id, node_attestation_id, issuer_key_id, and
    binary_artifact_id fields so the pipeline carries the IDs needed
    for concrete revocation lookups.
  - EnforcementConfig gains revocation_registry:
    Option<Arc<RevocationRegistry>>, letting callers inject the live
    registry without threading it through every call site.
  - The RevocationCheck stage now performs BOTH the existing
    freshness validation AND `is_revoked()` lookups for all four
    artifact-ID categories; a hit on any lookup short-circuits the
    decision to Deny.

- crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs
  - Imports updated (PolicyPattern, UsageMetric, UsageMetricKind) to
    back the expanded context builder.
  - validate_admin_authorization now parses the Authorization header
    with a strict scheme+token split instead of a loose "Bearer "
    prefix strip. Rejects headers with extra whitespace, missing
    token, or extra trailing tokens; matches the scheme
    case-insensitively per RFC 7235.
  - Wires the host-held RevocationRegistry into EnforcementConfig so
    every request served by this binary passes through the concrete
    revocation check.

Net effect: revoked credentials are now rejected at the host boundary
even when the local revocation list is fresh, matching the documented
security contract of the 11-stage pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
